description_long: This is a large, double-headed battle axe with a long, slightly curved wooden handle. The axe features two asymmetrical steel blades, one of which is larger and more ornate, with a jagged lower edge and a pointed tip, while the other is smaller and more utilitarian. The head of the axe is topped with a sharp spear-like spike, adding to its versatility as both a cutting and thrusting weapon. Decorative metal bands and leather wrappings reinforce the handle near the head, and a small chain with metallic ornaments dangles from one side. The wood of the handle appears aged and robust, with a natural grain and a reddish-brown hue. The overall design suggests a medieval or fantasy aesthetic, suitable for display or ceremonial use as well as practical application.
description: A large, ornate double-headed battle axe with a long, curved wooden handle, steel blades, and decorative metal accents. Features a spear-like spike on top and a dangling chain ornament.
description_view_0: The main blade and spike are clearly visible, showing the ornate, jagged edge and the chain ornament hanging from the head.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed edge-on, revealing the thin profile of the blades and the length of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the axe head is visible, showing the smaller blade and the curvature of the handle.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, highlighting the symmetry of the blades and the reinforced handle near the head.
attribute_handle_type: Long, slightly curved wooden handle with visible grain, reinforced with leather wrappings and metal bands near the head; round cross-section, ergonomic grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed steel blade with one large, ornate, jagged-edged side and one smaller, simpler side; spear-like spike on top; decorative cutouts and a chain ornament.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and pinned mounting with visible metal bands and rivets securing the blade to the handle.
